rulings.Case Title: ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION, A NON-PROFIT VS. CALIFORNIA STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD, A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Second Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 01, 2025, Line 1. 2 - RESPONDENTs CALIFORNIA STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD,and ERIC OPEENHEIMER's Motion To Strike Portions Of The Second Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief. Continued to May 19, 2025, on the court's motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JESSE TORRES VS. NEUTRON HOLDINGS, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Deeming Admitted The Matters Specified In Requests For Admissions, Set Two; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 01, 2025, Line 3. 1 - DEFENDANT NEUTRON HOLDINGS, INC. DBA LIME's Motion For An Order Deeming Admitted The Matters Specified In Requests For Admissions, Set Two. Moving party shall provide a proposed order on EACH motion. 1. Defendant's Motion for an Order Deeming Admitted Its Requests for Admissions is GRANTED. The discovery requests at issue appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has not established that he has provided any responses to the requests for admission (RFAs). No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for requests for admissions when no responses have been provided. (CCP Section 2030.290.) Yet, Defendant has shown that it did meet and confer, to no avail. All requests in Set Two addressed to Plaintiff Jesse Torres (nos. 62-84) are deemed admitted. Plaintiff did not act with substantial justification in connection with these requests. Plaintiff shall pay $150 to the Clerk of the Court as sanctions, payment no later than June 3, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
